qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded recursion in ASN.1 parsing allows denial-of-service via crafted deeply-nested structures
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies unbounded recursion in ASN1InputStream/ASN1StreamParser (core module) causing StackOverflowError, while the advisory describes unbounded PGP AEAD chunk size in bcpg modules causing resource exhaustion. Different root cause, different vulnerable code (core/src/main/java/org/bouncycastle/asn1/ vs pg/ AEAD processing), and different exploitation mechanism (recursive stack overflow vs unbounded memory allocation from large AEAD chunk sizes).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory (GHSA-cj8j-37rh-8475) describes unbounded PGP AEAD chunk size in bcpg modules leading to pre-auth resource exhaustion, with fix commit dc7530939ffb6cdb57636f3609d98e23b94e71c1. The only qualifying finding identifies unbounded recursion in ASN1InputStream/ASN1StreamParser in the core module (asn1-deep-nesting-stack-overflow), which is a different vulnerability mechanism (stack overflow from recursive parsing vs memory exhaustion from large AEAD chunk allocation), different module (core vs bcpg), and different vulnerable code path.
evidence: Advisory summary: 'Unbounded PGP AEAD chunk size leads to pre-auth resource exhaustion' affecting bcpg modules
Advisory affected packages: org.bouncycastle:bcpg-jdk14, bcpg-jdk15to18, bcpg-jdk18on (all PGP modules)
Advisory fix commit: dc7530939ffb6cdb57636f3609d98e23b94e71c1 (PGP AEAD-related fix)
Finding code locations: core/src/main/java/org/bouncycastle/asn1/ASN1InputStream.java and ASN1StreamParser.java (core ASN.1 module, not bcpg)
Finding root cause: recursive ASN1InputStream.readVector() creating new instances per nesting level with no depth limit
Advisory root cause: unbounded PGP AEAD chunk size allocation without limits
